## [00:00 - 01:32]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're going to talk about how the future of search could impact content creation. Let's go! So what happens to content creation if the search engines of the future become answer engines? This is something that I've been grappling with as someone who has benefited significantly from referral traffic from Google. So let's assume for a moment that search engines become answer engines and that referral traffic becomes a thing of the past. Maybe it doesn't disappear completely, but it drops a lot. I would assume that that's going to lower the motivation for a lot of content creators when it comes to creating new content on their own websites. And certainly you can drive traffic other ways, but how would that drop impact our strategies? I assume that's going to result in businesses going to where their customers are at far more often. Social media sites in particular. It also feels like an endless cycle of problems because if suddenly we're creating less content on our websites, what's the data that's still feeding these search engines? What happens to news sites? Are they still okay? Anyway, I don't have a crystal ball. Lots of thoughts. Follow from more. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok.
